quests.computer.subtitle: A very powerful Lua scripting mod
quests.computer.cc_guide.title: Welcome to ComputerCraft
quests.computer.cc_guide.description:
Finding traditional automation methods a bit monotonous? ComputerCraft (CC) introduces a fundamentally different approach. As you advance into the &6HV Era&f, computers will become essential for building more flexible and powerful automation systems—though mastering them does require some patience and learning.

This chapter covers the basic functions of &eCC items&f. To truly unlock their potential, however, we recommend learning the basics of &bLua&f programming later on.
quests.computer.disks.title: Floppy Disk
quests.computer.disks.subtitle: Typing it all out again? There's a better way.
quests.computer.disks.description: Floppy disks are used to share programs between different computers (typically with a capacity of 125KB). Simply save the files onto a floppy disk, then insert it into another computer's disk drive to read them—much like using a dedicated program USB drive.
quests.computer.computers.title: Computer
quests.computer.computers.subtitle: It's not a bug,it's a feature.
quests.computer.computers.description:
A ComputerCraft computer can be placed anywhere and used by right-clicking to open its interface. Inside, you can use the edit <filename> command to write and run Lua programs.

Beyond that, computers can integrate deeply with other mods. Through programming, you can make the computer output a redstone signal of specific strength from any of its six sides, allowing you to control machines or circuits.

Programs you write can be saved onto a floppy disk for easy portability and use on other computers.
quests.computer.pocket_computer.title: Pocket Computer
quests.computer.pocket_computer.descript: Note that a Pocket Computer cannot directly output Redstone signals and must be equipped with a wireless modem to remotely control other devices.
quests.computer.turtle.title: Turtle
quests.computer.turtle.subtitle: A turtle in the sea?
quests.computer.turtle.description:
A Turtle is a specialized computer capable of performing world-interaction tasks on behalf of the player (such as mining, logging, etc.). Before activating a Turtle, you must equip it with tools, fuel, and an executable program.

It's worth noting that the name &b'Turtle'&f originates from the Logo programming language in the 1960s, specifically its turtle graphics system. Later, Python programmers developed a library called 'Turtle' (a collection of reusable code), which allows users to implement turtle graphics in Python.
quests.computer.wireless_modem.title: Wireless Modem
quests.computer.wireless_modem.descripti:
A Wireless Modem can transmit messages within a limited range.

When its Y-coordinate is <96, a Wireless Modem can only send messages to other modems (including Ender Modems) within a 64-block radius.

The transmission range gradually increases starting from Y>=96. At the maximum world height, the range reaches a radius of 384 blocks.
quests.computer.turtle_advanced.title: Advanced Turtle
quests.computer.turtle_advanced.descript: Want to keep track of your turtle at all times? It’s best to equip it with a wireless modem in case it "gets lost."
quests.computer.basic_red.title: Simple Redstone Circuit
quests.computer.basic_red.description: The simplest and most commonly used Redstone circuit.
quests.computer.basic_red.subtitle: A Simple Start
quests.computer.intermediate_red.title: Intermediate Redstone Circuit
quests.computer.advanced_red.title: Advanced Redstone Circuit
quests.computer.advanced_red.description: More advanced circuits that can simultaneously receive Redstone signals from different colored wires.
quests.computer.wired_modem.title: Wired Modem
quests.computer.wired_modem.description: There are two ways to connect peripheral devices: first, by placing them directly adjacent to the computer; second, by using a Wired Modem.
quests.computer.ender_modem.title: Ender Modem
quests.computer.ender_modem.description: The Ender Modem is an upgraded version of the Wireless Modem, capable of transmitting messages to other wireless modems over any distance—even across dimensions. This allows you to build a truly robust computer network.
quests.computer.ender_modem.subtitle: Upgrade!
